Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|12=draft|12=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|12=draft|12=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|12=draft|12=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=draft|12=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|12=draft|12=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|12=draft|12=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|12=draft|12=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|12=draft|12=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=draft|12=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|12=draft|12=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|12=draft|12=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|12=draft|12=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Egyptian Deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|12=draft|12=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=draft|12=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=draft|12=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=draft|12=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=draft|12=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|12=draft|12=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|12=draft|12=draft}} would imply. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|12=draft|12=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|12=draft|12=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=draft|12=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=draft|12=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=draft|12=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=draft|12=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|12=draft|12=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|12=draft|12=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|12=draft|12=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|12=draft|12=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=draft|12=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|12=draft|12=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|12=draft|12=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|12=draft|12=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|12=draft|12=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=draft|12=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|12=draft|12=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|12=draft|12=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|12=draft|12=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]]

An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|12=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|12=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|12=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|12=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|12=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|12=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|12=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|12=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|12=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|12=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|12=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|12=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]]

{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]]

Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]]

Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.comment: Bot: mark ja import issue
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]]

Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]]

Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}

Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|de|Nilschwemme|ja|ニルシュウェンメ|lt=Flooding of the Nile|lt_ja=ニルシュウェンメ|lt_de=Nilschwemme|qq=draft}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|de|Nil|ja|ナイル川|lt=Nile|lt_ja=ナイル川|lt_de=Nil|qq=draft}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|island|de|Insel|ja|島|lt=Nile islands|lt_ja=ナイルの島々|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|qq=draft}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|reed|de|Schilfrohr|ja|葦|lt=reeds|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|rushes|de|Röhricht|ja|葦|lt=rushes|lt_ja=葦|lt_de=Röhricht|qq=draft}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|desert|de|Wüste|ja|砂漠|lt=desert|lt_ja=砂漠|lt_de=Wüste|qq=draft}} fringe areas: {{ill|pond|de|Tümpel|ja|池|lt=pond|lt_ja=池|lt_de=Tümpel|qq=draft}}, {{ill|oasis|de|Oase|ja|オアシス|lt=oasis|lt_ja=オアシス|lt_de=Oase|qq=draft}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|cultivated soil|de|Kulturboden|ja|耕作土壌|lt=arable land|lt_ja=耕作土壌|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|rainy season|de|Regenzeit|ja|雨季|lt=rainy season|lt_ja=雨季|lt_de=Regenzeit|qq=draft}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|topographical|de|Topografie (Kartografie)|ja|地形学的|lt=topographical|lt_ja=地形学的|lt_de=topografische|qq=draft}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|ja|古代エジプト|lt=Egyptians|lt_ja=エジプト人|lt_de=Ägypter|qq=draft}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=mythological interpretation|lt_ja=神話的解釈|lt_de=mythologische Auslegung|qq=draft}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|qq=draft}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|lt=Bertha Porter|lt_ja=バーサ・ポーター|lt_de=Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=ロザリンド・モス|lt_de=Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sechet-tjau]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sechet-tjau]] [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|qq=draft}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|landscape|de|Landschaft|ja|風景|lt=fields|lt_ja=野原|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|ja|エジプト神話|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=エジプト神話|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft}}, a part of the region {{ill|Sechet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|ja|セシェト・イヤル|lt=Sechet-iaru|lt_ja=セシェト・イヤル|lt_de=Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|qq=draft}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|lt=Duat|lt_ja=ドゥアト|lt_de=Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|qq=draft}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|qq=draft}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|ja|エジプト死者の書|lt=Book of the Dead spells|lt_ja=エジプト死者の書の呪文|lt_de=Totenbuchsprüchen|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom|de|Neues Reich|ja|新王国|lt=New Kingdom|lt_ja=新王国|lt_de=Neuen Reichs|qq=draft}}. Thus, in the {{ill|18th Dynasty|de|18. Dynastie|ja|第18王朝|lt=18th Dynasty|lt_ja=第18王朝|lt_de=18. Dynastie|qq=draft}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|de|Erik Hornung|ja|エリック・ホルヌング|lt=Erik Hornung|lt_ja=エリック・ホルヌング|lt_de=Erik Hornung|qq=draft}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|suggestion|de|Suggestion|ja|提案|lt=suggested|lt_ja=提案された|lt_de=suggeriert|qq=draft}} would imply. In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''.
