[[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263582]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''[[:en:Landscape|fields]] of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Book of the Dead spells]] of the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]. Thus, in the [[:en:Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|18th Dynasty]], an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.[[:en:Erik Hornung|Erik Hornung]]: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as [[:en:Suggestion|suggested]] would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nile]], which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * [[:en:Island|Nile islands]]: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * [[:en:Phragmites australis|reeds]] areas: Sections of land with zones of [[:en:Reed bed|rushes]], found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|desert]] fringe areas: [[:en:Pond|pond]], [[:en:Oasis|oasis]] and only temporarily usable [[:en:Arable land|arable land]], which slowly dried up again after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|rainy season]]. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No [[:en:Topography|topographical]] description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Egyptians]], who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The [[:en:Egyptian mythology|mythological interpretation]] of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263582]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''[[:en:Landscape|fields]] of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Book of the Dead spells]] of the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]. Thus, in the [[:en:Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|18th Dynasty]], an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.[[:en:Erik Hornung|Erik Hornung]]: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as [[:en:Suggestion|suggested]] would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nile]], which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * [[:en:Island|Nile islands]]: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * [[:en:Phragmites australis|reeds]] areas: Sections of land with zones of [[:en:Reed bed|rushes]], found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|desert]] fringe areas: [[:en:Pond|pond]], [[:en:Oasis|oasis]] and only temporarily usable [[:en:Arable land|arable land]], which slowly dried up again after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|rainy season]]. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No [[:en:Topography|topographical]] description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Egyptians]], who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The [[:en:Egyptian mythology|mythological interpretation]] of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''[[:en:Landscape|fields]] of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Book of the Dead spells]] of the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]. Thus, in the [[:en:Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|18th Dynasty]], an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.[[:en:Erik Hornung|Erik Hornung]]: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as [[:en:Suggestion|suggested]] would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nile]], which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * [[:en:Island|Nile islands]]: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * [[:en:Phragmites australis|reeds]] areas: Sections of land with zones of [[:en:Reed bed|rushes]], found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|desert]] fringe areas: [[:en:Pond|pond]], [[:en:Oasis|oasis]] and only temporarily usable [[:en:Arable land|arable land]], which slowly dried up again after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|rainy season]]. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No [[:en:Topography|topographical]] description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Egyptians]], who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The [[:en:Egyptian mythology|mythological interpretation]] of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nile]], which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * [[:en:Island|Nile islands]]: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * [[:en:Phragmites australis|reeds]] areas: Sections of land with zones of [[:en:Reed bed|rushes]], found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|desert]] fringe areas: [[:en:Pond|pond]], [[:en:Oasis|oasis]] and only temporarily usable [[:en:Arable land|arable land]], which slowly dried up again after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|rainy season]]. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No [[:en:Topography|topographical]] description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Egyptians]], who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The [[:en:Egyptian mythology|mythological interpretation]] of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263582]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''[[:en:Landscape|fields]] of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]], a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Book of the Dead spells]] of the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]]. Thus, in the [[:en:Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|18th Dynasty]], an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.[[:en:Erik Hornung|Erik Hornung]]: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as [[:en:Suggestion|suggested]] would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Flooding of the Nile]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nile]], which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * [[:en:Island|Nile islands]]: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * [[:en:Phragmites australis|reeds]] areas: Sections of land with zones of [[:en:Reed bed|rushes]], found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|desert]] fringe areas: [[:en:Pond|pond]], [[:en:Oasis|oasis]] and only temporarily usable [[:en:Arable land|arable land]], which slowly dried up again after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|rainy season]]. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No [[:en:Topography|topographical]] description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Egyptians]], who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The [[:en:Egyptian mythology|mythological interpretation]] of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263582]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|Landscape|en|Landscape|WD=Q107425|lt=fields}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology}}, a part of the region {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|WD=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|en|Book of the Dead|WD=Q174361|lt=Book of the Dead spells}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}. Thus, in the {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q146055|lt=18th Dynasty}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|en|Erik Hornung|WD=Q117212|lt=Erik Hornung}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|Suggestion|en|Suggestion|WD=Q690194|lt=suggested}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Nile islands}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|Phragmites australis|en|Phragmites australis|WD=Q28557|lt=reeds}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=rushes}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|Desert|en|Desert|WD=Q8514|lt=desert}} fringe areas: {{ill|Pond|en|Pond|WD=Q12719646|lt=pond}}, {{ill|Oasis|en|Oasis|WD=Q43742|lt=oasis}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|Arable land|en|Arable land|WD=Q843920|lt=arable land}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|Wet season|en|Wet season|WD=Q3117517|lt=rainy season}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|Topography|en|Topography|WD=Q134435|lt=topographical}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Egyptians}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=mythological interpretation}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|en|Rosalind Moss|WD=Q15130156|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|en|Rosalind Moss|WD=Q15130156|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|Landscape|en|Landscape|WD=Q107425|lt=fields}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology}}, a part of the region {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|WD=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|en|Book of the Dead|WD=Q174361|lt=Book of the Dead spells}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}. Thus, in the {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q146055|lt=18th Dynasty}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|en|Erik Hornung|WD=Q117212|lt=Erik Hornung}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|Suggestion|en|Suggestion|WD=Q690194|lt=suggested}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Nile islands}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|Phragmites australis|en|Phragmites australis|WD=Q28557|lt=reeds}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=rushes}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|Desert|en|Desert|WD=Q8514|lt=desert}} fringe areas: {{ill|Pond|en|Pond|WD=Q12719646|lt=pond}}, {{ill|Oasis|en|Oasis|WD=Q43742|lt=oasis}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|Arable land|en|Arable land|WD=Q843920|lt=arable land}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|Wet season|en|Wet season|WD=Q3117517|lt=rainy season}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|Topography|en|Topography|WD=Q134435|lt=topographical}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Egyptians}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=mythological interpretation}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|en|Rosalind Moss|WD=Q15130156|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Egyptians|1=Ancient Egypt}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=mythological interpretation|1=Egyptian mythology}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter|1=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|en|Rosalind Moss|WD=Q15130156|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|1=Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = R4-G17-V30 M20 P5-G17-D19:I9
Hotep-em-neb-sechet-tjau-em-fendjef
''Htp-m-nb-sḫt-Ṯ3w-m-fnḏ=f''
''To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME2 = M20-P5-N35:N25-X1:O49
Sechet-tjau
''Sḫt-Ṯ3w''
''{{ill|Landscape|en|Landscape|WD=Q107425|lt=fields|1=Landscape}} of Breathing'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Sechet-tjau''' (short form also: '''Tjau''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}, a part of the region {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|WD=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru}} in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|タ・デジェル|lt=Ta-djeser|lt_ja=タ・デジェル|lt_de=Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which lies in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}. == Background == The {{ill|Identification|de|Identifikation|ja|アイデンティフィケーション|lt=Identification|lt_ja=アイデンティフィケーション|lt_de=Identifikation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Identification|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the region Sechet-tjau originated in the {{ill|Late Period|de|Spätzeit (Ägypten)|ja|後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|lt_de=Spätzeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is based on a misinterpreted reading of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|en|Book of the Dead|WD=Q174361|lt=Book of the Dead spells|1=Book of the Dead}} of the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}. Thus, in the {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q146055|lt=18th Dynasty|1=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt}}, an inscription concerning Sechet-iaru read: “To be Hotep, the Lord of the Field (with) Breath on his Nose”.{{ill|Erik Hornung|en|Erik Hornung|WD=Q117212|lt=Erik Hornung|1=Erik Hornung}}: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead''. Artemis und Winkler, Düsseldorf 2000, ISBN 3-7608-1201-5, p. 217. Additionally, the Book of the Dead spells contained further passages such as “Wind on his Nose”, which led to the assumption that Sechet-tjau must be an area of the north wind or of breathing. == Meaning of the Term == The designation Sechet-tjau consists of two independent word meanings. In this context, various interpretations do not allow for a definitive assignment. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for “field” or “fields”, encompassing a large region and can therefore also be translated as “landscape”. An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not accurate, as that would suggest an area too small – as {{ill|Suggestion|en|Suggestion|WD=Q690194|lt=suggested|1=Suggestion}} would imply. The term Sechet referred to several landscape areas: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile|1=Flooding of the Nile}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}}, which were inundated by the annual Nile flood. * {{ill|Island|en|Island|WD=Q23442|lt=Nile islands|1=Island}}: Land masses lying above the {{ill|water level|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|水位|lt=water level|lt_ja=水位|lt_de=Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water level|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高さ|1=Height|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q208826}} in the Nile, which could be used agriculturally as enclosed areas. * {{ill|Phragmites australis|en|Phragmites australis|WD=Q28557|lt=reeds|1=Phragmites australis}} areas: Sections of land with zones of {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=rushes|1=Reed bed}}, found at standing and slowly flowing waters up to a meter deep. * {{ill|Desert|en|Desert|WD=Q8514|lt=desert|1=Desert}} fringe areas: {{ill|Pond|en|Pond|WD=Q12719646|lt=pond|1=Pond}}, {{ill|Oasis|en|Oasis|WD=Q43742|lt=oasis|1=Oasis}} and only temporarily usable {{ill|Arable land|en|Arable land|WD=Q843920|lt=arable land|1=Arable land}}, which slowly dried up again after the end of the {{ill|Wet season|en|Wet season|WD=Q3117517|lt=rainy season|1=Wet season}}. === Tjau === Tjau means “breath” or “wind” and is understood, in relation to the region Sechet-iaru, as a sub-area. A literal translation must remain open-ended, as the term Tjau was used in a very multifaceted way. == Location in Sechet-iaru == No {{ill|Topography|en|Topography|WD=Q134435|lt=topographical|1=Topography}} description of Sechet-tjau exists. In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Egyptians|1=Ancient Egypt}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=mythological interpretation|1=Egyptian mythology}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''Reports of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen, No. 8''. Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|lt=Rolf Krauss|lt_ja=ロルフ・クラウス|lt_de=Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Conceptions of the Afterlife in the Pyramid Texts''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter|1=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|en|Rosalind Moss|WD=Q15130156|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|1=Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings; Vol. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs''. Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. == References == {{translated page|de|Sechet-tjau|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セシェト・トジャウ]] [[de:Sechet-tjau]] {{wikidata link|Q2263582}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

In the Late Period, the interpretations of the inscription could evidently not be correctly deciphered, leading to the assumption that it must be an independent region. These conclusions likely refer to the real life of the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Egyptians|1=Ancient Egypt}}, who understood the wind as the breath of the gods. The {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|WD=Q205740|lt=mythological interpretation|1=Egyptian mythology}} of breathing was certainly the reason to suspect a region of Breathing in the Duat. Since a secure understanding of the inscription was lacking in the Late Period, the Egyptians, from the beginning of that period, regarded Sechet-tjau as a necessary component of Sechet-iaru, indispensable for the existence of the deceased and their provision with the necessary breath as wind. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-hetep|de|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテップ|lt=Sechet-hetep|lt_ja=セシェト・ヘテップ|lt_de=Sechet-hetep|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|メレンチャ|lt=Merencha|lt_ja=メレンチャ|lt_de=Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|エルマー・エーデル|lt=Elmar Edel|lt_ja=エルマー・エーデル|lt_de=Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.''
